name: Claude Design
summary: text-to-visual prototyping tool that generates mockups, slides, and one-pagers from prompts — reads your codebase and design files for brand consistency, exports to PDF/PPTX/Canva; available in Claude Pro/Max

name: Claude Opus 4.7
summary: Anthropic's best generally available model — 3x higher vision resolution (3.75MP), 1M token context, new 'xhigh' effort level, 64% on SWE-Bench Pro; same pricing as Opus 4.6

name: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS
summary: expressive text-to-speech API available now in Gemini API and AI Studio — 200+ audio tags for directing delivery ([slowly, with gravity]), 70-language support, SynthID watermarking; free tier available

name: HuggingFace + GitHub Copilot Chat
summary: open-source models now plug into GitHub Copilot Chat in VS Code — swap in Qwen, Llama, or any HF model as your in-editor AI assistant without leaving your IDE

name: Claude Managed Agents + ant CLI
summary: public beta for building and deploying Claude agents — sandboxed execution, checkpointing, scoped permissions, SSE streaming; new ant CLI lets you ship agents from the terminal

name: Google AI Edge Eloquent
summary: free offline-first voice dictation app for iOS powered by on-device Gemma — transcribes in real time, strips filler words, reformats to bullet points or formal prose; no subscription, no cloud required

name: Qwen 3
summary: two Apache 2.0 releases: 72B dense and 235B MoE — both free to run locally, rival GPT-class performance for coding and reasoning without cloud costs

name: Llama 4 Scout + Maverick
summary: Meta's first natively multimodal open models — Scout (17B active/109B total MoE, runs on a single H100) and Maverick (17B active/400B total, beats GPT-4o on most benchmarks); video and image understanding, Apache 2.0

name: OpenAI o3 + o4-mini
summary: o3 matches or beats o1 on most benchmarks; o4-mini is the fastest reasoning model available via API — both support image input, web search, and tool use; o3 available in ChatGPT Plus/Pro

name: GPT-4.1
summary: API-only model with 1M token context and significantly better instruction following than GPT-4o — strong coding benchmark results, cheaper than GPT-4o; includes mini and nano variants

name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
summary: thinking model that leads on reasoning and coding benchmarks — deep 1M context, runs natively on video and audio, available in AI Studio as an experimental preview before general release

name: GPT-4.5
summary: OpenAI's largest pre-o1 model — significantly stronger emotional intelligence and conversational naturalness, though not a reasoning model; released as a research preview before broader rollout

name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
summary: first Claude model with extended thinking — toggle on to see reasoning traces up to 64K tokens; leads on SWE-bench and Aider polyglot coding benchmarks; computer use capability now available in the API

name: Grok 3
summary: xAI's flagship model trained on 200K H100 cluster — beats GPT-4o and Gemini 2 on math and science benchmarks; includes a fast 'mini' variant and DeepSearch mode for live web browsing

name: DeepSeek R1
summary: fully open reasoning model (MIT license) that matches o1 on math and code — training cost under $6M; triggered a major market conversation about US–China AI competition and Nvidia stock selloff

name: DeepSeek V3
summary: 671B MoE open-weights model from China — tops most code and math benchmarks, trained for ~$6M total; runs on consumer hardware via quantized versions; raised major questions about compute efficiency

name: Sora
summary: text-to-video model finally available to the public after a year of previews — up to 1080p/20s clips, image-to-video, video extension; built-in C2PA watermarking; $20/mo for 50 priority videos

name: Gemini 2.0 Flash
summary: 2x faster than 1.5 Pro at similar quality — first Gemini model with native image and audio output; available in AI Studio with Multimodal Live API for real-time streaming

name: Claude 3.5 Sonnet v2 + Computer Use
summary: updated Sonnet outperforms Claude 3 Opus on coding benchmarks; introduces Computer Use API beta — Claude can control a desktop, browse the web, and run code autonomously via screenshots

name: Claude 3.5 Haiku
summary: fastest model in the Claude 3.5 family — matches Claude 3 Opus performance at a fraction of the cost; strong on coding with text-only input; vision support rolling out in November

name: OpenAI o1
summary: first publicly released reasoning model from OpenAI — 'thinks before it answers' using chain-of-thought; PhDs on hard math and science; much slower and pricier than GPT-4o by design

name: Mistral Large 2
summary: 123B parameter model with 128K context — strong code generation, multi-step reasoning, and 80-language support; released under a research license with API access; challenges GPT-4o on code benchmarks

name: GPT-4o mini
summary: replaces GPT-3.5 Turbo across the API — smarter, faster, and 60% cheaper; handles text and vision; small models race effectively over as OpenAI's primary low-cost tier

name: Claude 3.5 Sonnet
summary: first Claude 3.5 model — outperforms Claude 3 Opus at 2× the speed; sets new records on coding (HumanEval 92%), grad-level reasoning, and vision; free on Claude.ai, available in the API

name: GPT-4o
summary: omni model that unifies text, vision, and audio natively — real-time voice with emotions, reads facial expressions, 2× faster and 50% cheaper than GPT-4 Turbo; available free in ChatGPT

name: Llama 3
summary: two open-weights models: 8B and 70B — both fine-tuned on 15T tokens, outperform Gemma and Mistral at similar scale; 4x bigger pre-training dataset than Llama 2; Apache 2.0 license
